name: GameAssist
description:
**A modular D&D 5E (2014) automation suite for Roll20, covering critical misses, concentration checks, NPC hit points, encounter history, configuration, and diagnostics.**

GameAssist v0.1.4.7 combines six individually configurable modules behind one shared interface. Use the complete suite or enable only the tools that fit your campaign.

---

## 🧭 What GameAssist Does

GameAssist reduces the routine steps surrounding combat and campaign recordkeeping. It can guide critical misses, build concentration saves, roll NPC hit points, mark defeated NPCs, and preserve encounter history in Roll20 handouts. Shared settings and status menus give DMs one place to control and troubleshoot these tools while each module remains independently configurable.

---

## ⚙️ Requirements

- **Character sheet:** D&D 5E (2014 Character Sheet)
- **Marker dependency:** TokenMod 0.8.88 is the tested standalone baseline for NPCManager and ConcentrationTracker marker changes. Newer compatible standalone builds should be smoke-tested.
- **Optional companion:** StatusInfo 0.3.11 for condition descriptions and menus
- **CritFumble tables:** `CF-Melee`, `CF-Ranged`, `CF-Thrown`, `CF-Spell`, `CF-Natural`, `Confirm-Crit-Martial`, and `Confirm-Crit-Magic`

> GameAssist does not create or populate campaign-specific CritFumble results automatically. The seven rollable tables must exist before those features can produce table results.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

1. Install **GameAssist** and **TokenMod**.
2. Create the seven CritFumble rollable tables if you plan to use CritFumble.
3. Save or restart the Roll20 Mod sandbox.
4. Run `!ga-status` to review overall health, module state, and dependencies.
5. Run `!ga-config ui` to open the GM control panel.

---

## 🧩 Included Modules

- **CritFumble** — Watches supported attack templates for natural 1s, presents player-facing choices, and rolls from melee, ranged, thrown, spell, or natural-attack fumble tables. GMs can also open the picker manually or roll martial and magic confirmation tables directly.
- **ConcentrationTracker** — Builds normal, advantage, or disadvantage Constitution saves from a supplied damage amount, remembers each player's latest check, manages the configured concentration marker through TokenMod, and reports concentrating tokens on the current player page.
- **NPCManager** — Marks defeated NPCs, records revivals, audits HP and marker mismatches, and organizes encounter history into readable handouts using **Campaign > Chapter > Section > Session** scopes. Custom Arc rosters can track selected NPCs across stories, vows, rivalries, investigations, redemption arcs, and other campaign threads.
- **NPCHPRoller** — Rolls `npc_hpformula` for selected NPCs or qualifying NPCs on the current page. Optional automatic rolling handles newly added tokens while setup protection prevents placeholder HP from producing false death or revival history.
- **ConfigUI** — Provides a GM-only chat interface for enabling modules, changing common settings, reviewing configuration, and reading module summaries. Command-based configuration remains available.
- **DebugTools** — Provides optional GM-only damage, marker, and saving-throw diagnostics. DebugTools starts disabled and defaults to dry-run previews unless the GM explicitly applies a change.

---

## 🎲 Example at the Table

When a linked NPC falls from positive HP to 0, NPCManager records the event in the active Campaign, Chapter, Section, and Session scopes and asks TokenMod to apply the configured death marker. If the NPC later rises above 0 HP, GameAssist records the revival and requests marker removal. The GM can review a recent chat summary, audit the current page, write records to handouts, or collect selected NPCs into a custom Arc roster.

---

## 💬 Main Commands

### System and Configuration

- `!ga-status` — Show overall health, module state, errors, and dependency information
- `!ga-status --details` — Add expanded activity, queue, listener, timing, and standalone-integration diagnostics
- `!ga-config ui` or `!ga-config-ui` — Open the GM configuration panel
- `!ga-config modules` — List modules and their current status
- `!ga-config list` — Write a versioned configuration-only snapshot handout
- `!ga-config get <Module> [key]` / `!ga-config set <Module> <key>=<value>` — Review or change module configuration
- `!ga-config cleanup` — Explicitly remove unknown or orphaned GameAssist state branches
- `!ga-enable <Module>` / `!ga-disable <Module>` — Enable or disable a module
- `!ga-metrics` — Show GameAssist runtime metrics

### Critical Misses

- `!critfumble help` — Open the CritFumble quick-reference guide
- `!critfumble menu` — Open the guided Natural 1 menu
- `!critfail` — Open the direct manual player picker
- `!critfumble-<melee|ranged|thrown|spell|natural>` — Roll the selected fumble table
- `!confirm-crit-martial` / `!confirm-crit-magic` — Roll confirmation tables

### Concentration

- `!concentration` or `!cc` — Open concentration-check buttons
- `!concentration --damage <amount> --mode <normal|adv|dis>` — Roll a concentration save
- `!concentration --last` — Repeat the player's previous check
- `!concentration --off` — Remove the configured marker from selected linked tokens
- `!concentration --status` — List concentrating tokens on the current player page
- `!concentration --config randomize on|off` — Toggle randomized result narration
- `!ga-conc-status` — Show the GM concentration activity summary

### NPC History and Reporting

- `!npc-death-report --help` or `!npc-death-help` — Open the central NPCManager guide
- `!npc-death-report [--scope campaign|chapter|section|session] [--recent|--page N|--write]` — Review a reporting scope or open the report writer
- `!npc-death-buckets` — Review or rename active reporting scopes
- `!npc-death-write` or `!npc-wr` — Write NPC history to handouts
- `!npc-death-audit` — Audit HP and death-marker consistency and update the audit handout
- `!npc-death-arc` — Create and manage custom Arc rosters from selected tokens or the current Session
- `!npc-death-clear [--scope campaign|chapter|section|session] [--nested] [--confirm]` — Clear records using explicit confirmation

### NPC Hit Points and Diagnostics

- `!npc-hp-selected` — Roll HP for selected qualifying NPCs
- `!npc-hp-all` — Roll HP for qualifying NPCs on the current page
- `!ga-debug` — Open diagnostics after enabling DebugTools

> GameAssist help panels explain available choices in plain language. The full README contains the complete command matrix, permissions, examples, module settings, report workflows, migration guidance, and troubleshooting procedures.

---

## 🛡️ Architecture and Safeguards

- Normal event handlers execute directly; serialized queue execution is explicit and opt-in.
- Health reports and bounded runtime metrics make stalled work or repeated errors easier to recognize.
- NPC setup protection prevents automatic HP initialization from creating false death or revival records.
- Marker requests are routed through standalone TokenMod and verified after the request.
- Built-in and custom status markers are resolved to the exact identifiers stored by Roll20.
- DebugTools is disabled by default and uses dry-run behavior unless a GM explicitly applies a change.
- Modules can be enabled or disabled independently without requiring the entire suite to be active.

---

## 🔗 Compatibility Notes

- **TokenMod 0.8.88** is the tested standalone baseline for NPCManager and ConcentrationTracker marker changes.
- **StatusInfo 0.3.11** is optional and can provide condition descriptions and menus alongside TokenMod.
- GameAssist treats token bar 1 as NPC HP for its NPC HP and death-history workflows.
- Avoid running duplicate standalone copies of GameAssist's bundled CritFumble, Concentration, NPC HP, or NPC death tools while the integrated modules are enabled.
- Features outside a disabled module, unavailable optional companion, or missing CritFumble table remain independently available.
- Compatibility scoring hints can be enabled through `GameAssist.flags.DEBUG_COMPAT` for advanced troubleshooting.

---

## 📚 Documentation and Support

See the [GameAssist README](https://github.com/Mord-Eagle/GameAssist#readme) for complete installation, configuration, command syntax, migration notes, troubleshooting, and upgrade guidance.

Bug reports and feature requests are welcome through [GameAssist Issues](https://github.com/Mord-Eagle/GameAssist/issues).
